qa_pairs.question: In 1988, white T-shirts with a single black stripe diagonally across them began to be sold in Italian markets. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: They were made to look like seatbelts so drivers could appear to be wearing a seatbelt without actually wearing one.

qa_pairs.question: As well as its olives and wine grapes, the residents of ancient Greece also valued another plant — the carob. It is said that its seeds had an unusual property that led to a useful quantity that we use today. What is it?
qa_pairs.answer: The carob seed gave rise to the carat, used as a unit for measuring diamonds.

qa_pairs.question: In which common commercial product do 25% of the contents have no effect?
qa_pairs.answer: A 28-day pack of birth control pills, where 7 of the pills are placebos.

qa_pairs.question: In 1991, a man lost his house and possessions in a large fire that spread through the Oakland Hills in San Francisco. As a direct result, he developed one of the biggest creative successes in history. What was it?
In 1960, Theodore Maiman defined the strength of these in terms of 'gillettes'. What were they?
In 2013, a Chinese man called Mr. Feng hired professional hitmen to kill his 23-year-old son, Xiao. Despite this becoming public knowledge, he was never charged with a crime. What was going on?
qa_pairs.answer: He hired professional gamers to repeatedly kill his son’s character in online video games.

qa_pairs.question: In which specific sport do you not cross the finish line, nor cross the start line either?
